steps.title: Find total area to cover
steps.content: The patio has d square feet. Covering it twice means a total coverage area of 2d square feet.

steps.title: Calculate gallons needed
steps.content: One gallon covers 240 sq ft, so gallons needed = $$\frac{2d}{240} = \frac{d}{120}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $c=\frac{240d}{27}$: This inverts the ratio - it multiplies by 240 instead of dividing, giving a cost far too large. Incorrect
  • Choice B — $c=\frac{480d}{27}$: Accounts for double coverage (480 = 2 × 240) in the numerator but the algebra is inverted - gallons should be 2d/240 = d/120, not 480d/27. Incorrect
  • Choice C — $c=27(\frac{d}{120})$: Divides by 120 (= 240/2) to account for double coverage, then multiplies by 27 dollars. This correctly gives c = 27d/120 = 54d/240, i.e. the cost for 2d sq ft at 27 dollars per 240 sq ft. Correct
  • Choice D — $c=27(\frac{d}{240})$: Gives the cost for covering the patio only once, not twice. Incorrect
This matches - C is correct.
